data_out.description: HDF5 TF2 model file with verified layer names (input_2048, input_4096, output)
Verification report listing confirmed layer names and structural compliance

domain_knowledge: HDF5 is the standard hierarchical data format for persisting TensorFlow/Keras models; TensorFlow 2.3.0 uses this format for compatibility with TensorFlow Serving.
Input layer names 'input_2048' and 'input_4096' conventionally refer to the dimensionality of input feature vectors expected by the model.
Layer name inspection in TensorFlow models is performed via the model.layers property or by iterating the functional graph; names must match exactly for downstream API routing.
The get_models.sh script automates model download and conversion, standardizing the pipeline and ensuring reproducibility across deployments.

direct_checks: verify file 'Classifier/models_folder/models/get_models.sh' exists in the repository
script_runs: execute get_models.sh in the Classifier/models_folder/models directory without errors
verify TensorFlow 2.3.0 is installed and importable as a Python module
file_exists: verify that an HDF5 model file (.h5 extension) is created after running get_models.sh
verify the HDF5 model can be loaded using tf.keras.models.load_model() without errors
verify the loaded model exposes a layer named exactly 'input_2048'
verify the loaded model exposes a layer named exactly 'input_4096'
verify the loaded model exposes a layer named exactly 'output'
verify the model has exactly three named layers matching the required names (no extraneous layers with those names)
expert_review: confirm that the HDF5 model structure is compatible with TensorFlow 2.3.0 format specifications
confirm that the layer names and connectivity represent a valid Keras classification model architecture suitable for natural product classification

finding: The NP Classifier model conversion process requires the resulting HDF5 TF2 model to expose three specific layer names: two input layers named 'input_2048' and 'input_4096', and one output layer named 'output'.
methodology_summary: Clone the NP-Classifier GitHub repository to obtain the model conversion script and pre-trained model artifacts.
Execute the get_models.sh script from Classifier/models_folder/models to download and convert Keras models to HDF5 TF2 format using TensorFlow 2.3.0.
Load the resulting HDF5 model into a TensorFlow 2.3.0 environment and programmatically inspect all layer objects.
Extract and validate layer names, confirming exact matches for 'input_2048', 'input_4096', and 'output'.
Validation: layer name inspection must report all three required layers with exact spelling and no additional input or output layers deviating from the specification.

task_description: Using the get_models.sh script and TensorFlow 2.3.0, convert Keras classification models to HDF5 TF2 format and verify that the resulting model exposes input layers named 'input_2048' and 'input_4096', plus an output layer named 'output'.
task_objective: Convert Keras models to HDF5 TF2 format using TensorFlow 2.3.0 and verify that input and output layer names match the required specification.
title: Reconstruct the Keras model conversion step producing a HDF5 TF2 model with named input/output layers

workflow_description: 1. Clone the NP-Classifier repository from mwang87/NP-Classifier. 2. Navigate to Classifier/models_folder/models and execute the get_models.sh script to download and convert the pre-trained Keras models. 3. Load the converted HDF5 TF2 model using TensorFlow 2.3.0's model loading API. 4. Inspect the model's layer architecture to confirm the presence of input layers named 'input_2048' and 'input_4096' and an output layer named 'output'. 5. Log or save the layer names and confirm structural compliance with the specification.
